# Deep Method Selection Diagnostics

Converted from `37_deep_method_selection_diagnostics.csv` so the pack remains Markdown-only.

| situation | best_methods | avoid | why | mindset | metrics |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Low awareness / no category belief | Educational content, founder-led insight, problem framing ads, category narrative, reports, diagnostic tools | Do not push demo too early | The buyer must first understand why the old way is costly and why the category exists. | Teacher of change | Problem-aware traffic, content depth, branded/category search lift, returning visitors |
| Problem-aware but not solution-aware | Search content, pain-to-workflow demos, calculators, webinars, checklists, lead magnets | Do not lead with vendor comparison before the buyer accepts the solution category | You are helping the buyer name and evaluate the solution class. | Guide | Lead quality, content-to-demo rate, checklist completion, return visits |
| Solution-aware but vendor-neutral | Comparison pages, review strategy, buyer guides, retargeting, product demos, case studies | Do not hide tradeoffs or pretend you are best for everyone | The buyer is building a shortlist; useful proof beats noise. | Trusted advisor | Comparison visits, pricing visits, demo/trial starts, shortlist mentions |
| Vendor-aware but skeptical | Proof architecture, security page, transparent pricing, interactive demo, sandbox, case studies, ROI model | Do not ask for belief; show evidence | The buyer needs risk reduction and control. | Evidence architect | Trial-to-paid, demo-to-opportunity, sales objections reduced, security packet downloads |
| Trial user not activated | Lifecycle emails, in-app nudges, onboarding checklist, demo data, support concierge | Do not upsell before value | The user must experience the first valuable outcome. | Adoption coach | Activation, time-to-value, feature completion, retained trials |
| Activated free/trial user not paying | Usage limits, value recap, upgrade prompts, team invite, billing clarity, risk reversal | Do not create artificial pain or lock data hostage | Make the paid boundary correspond to increased value. | Value accountant | Free-to-paid, expansion intent, plan-fit accuracy, churn/refund |
| Paid user at expansion point | Customer success review, usage insights, team workflows, case studies by role, executive reports | Do not expand without proof of achieved value | Expansion is credible when it follows demonstrated success. | Steward of value | NRR, expansion rate, product depth, customer satisfaction |
| High traffic, low conversion | Landing-page proof path, message testing, offer refinement, speed/usability, CTA alignment | Do not assume copy is the problem if traffic is wrong | Find the leak: relevance, proof, friction, or audience mismatch. | Conversion diagnostician | CVR, bounce, scroll depth, lead quality, downstream activation |
| High clicks, low quality | Audience narrowing, negative keywords, stronger qualification, problem-specific copy, pricing transparency | Do not optimize for CTR | Clicks are not buyers. Raise relevance and disqualify wrong-fit traffic. | Traffic filter | Qualified conversion, CAC, SQL rate, activation |
| High demo interest, low close rate | Objection mapping, sales enablement, proof assets, procurement/security support, better discovery | Do not patch sales with pressure tactics | The system is creating interest but not enough confidence. | Risk reducer | Demo-to-close, sales cycle, lost reason themes |
| Users churn after purchase | Onboarding, expectation alignment, customer education, product fit cleanup, success checkpoints | Do not replace retention work with more acquisition | Churn often means the promise and product reality are misaligned. | Promise auditor | Retention, time-to-value, support burden, expectation gaps |
| Strong product but low discoverability | SEO/GEO, earned media, reviews, partnerships, marketplaces, integration pages | Do not make thin AI content | The product needs to become findable in human and machine research paths. | Visibility architect | Search visibility, AI citations, review mentions, referral quality |
| Complex B2B buying committee | Stakeholder-specific assets, business case, security docs, IT/admin guide, internal champion deck | Do not rely on one champion to sell internally alone | Different stakeholders buy different risk reductions. | Committee architect | Stakeholder engagement, asset shares, deal velocity, procurement progression |
| Skeptical technical buyer | Docs, API examples, sandbox, benchmarks, changelog, comparison, architecture diagrams | Do not use vague benefit copy | Technical buyers trust working proof and control. | Builder showing work | Docs-to-signup, time-to-first-success, GitHub/docs engagement |
| AI product / AI feature | Capability demos, limitations, evaluation, human control, data policy, use-case-specific copy | Do not sell magic or guaranteed outcomes | AI trust comes from capability + control + limits. | Transparent operator | AI feature adoption, trust feedback, error recovery, retention |
